purpose: Interactive xterm.js terminal backed by a node-pty PTY in the Electron main process. Spawns a shell via api.spawnTerminal({cwd, dashboardId}), routes user input → api.writeTerminal(id, data), and PTY output → terminal.write(data) via 'terminal-output' push events. Handles 'terminal-exit' by displaying an [exit] message and waiting for any keypress to respawn. Uses FitAddon + ResizeObserver to auto-resize and informs the PTY of new dimensions via api.resizeTerminal. Theme is computed from CSS custom properties at mount and re-read on data-theme/style mutation observed on documentElement.
gotchas: Initialization effect has empty deps and is comment-tagged 'Mount once — projectDir changes don't re-create the terminal'. ProjectDir changes only re-fit; switching projectDir does NOT spawn a new shell in the new directory — the user is still in the original cwd.
isDisposedRef prevents writes after unmount, but xterm internals can still throw if `terminal.dispose()` runs while a write is in flight; the code wraps fit() in try/catch but not write().
`spawnRef` indirection lets exit-restart and effects call the latest spawnTerminal closure without breaking React rules — required because spawnTerminal depends on projectDir/dashboardId.
Theme MutationObserver fires on style changes on documentElement (e.g. --sidebar-width updates from Sidebar) — re-reads theme from CSS even when nothing visual changed; throttling not implemented.
Fallback `terminal-fallback` rendered when window.electronAPI is missing — browser SSE-only mode shows this instead of the terminal.
patterns: Refs for everything that should not trigger re-renders: terminalRef, fitAddonRef, terminalIdRef, isDisposedRef, spawnRef.
ResizeObserver tied to the container; on observe-fire it fits AND notifies PTY of cols/rows so the shell wraps correctly.
Restart-on-keypress pattern: terminal.onData() handler re-arms only when `exited` becomes true.
conventions: TERMINAL_OPTIONS at module scope; theme function (getTerminalThemeFromCSS) is called both at definition time and on mutation.
Shell prompt rendering is the PTY's job — TerminalView never injects prompt strings.
